headline: Venezuelans call for help to rescue survivors from rubble after twin earthquakes
description: International aid has only just begun to arrive, while local residents have been trying to clear the rubble with their bare hands since Wednesday. As the hours go by, the death toll of 920, along with 50,000 missing, is likely to rise.

headline: In Ukraine, those who leave and those who stay: Life on hold in Sloviansk and Kramatorsk
Lebanon and Israel sign framework agreement in Washington for 'lasting peace and security'
France heatwave: Why adaptation and mitigation must go hand in hand
description: To respond to climate change, authorities must urgently implement policies to make rising temperatures bearable for the public, without abandoning addressing the root cause of climate change.

headline: From a Russian prison to a European music festival, the escape of a young anti-Putin singer
description: On June 12, artist Diana Loginova performed among the stars of Russia's dissident music scene at the Outloud festival in Warsaw. She took to the stage after managing to escape from Russia. In 2025, she was in prison for singing songs condemned by the Kremlin.

headline: Dembélé shines at World Cup, silencing critics
description: After scoring a hat trick and named man of the match as France defeated Norway 4-1 on Friday, Ousmane Dembélé is making an impact at the 2026 World Cup. The long-awaited breakthrough with the French national team has finally arrived.

headline: 'They have the same criminal mentality': One year after replacing Wagner, Russia's Africa Corps steps up abuses in Mali
description: 'Mali: One year of Africa Corps' (2/2). Although directly overseen by the Russian Ministry of Defense, Africa Corps soldiers have already been implicated in numerous executions, sometimes accompanied by gruesome staging.

Sleeping in a cellar, waiting at a funeral home, riding the tram: How the French are improvising to survive the heatwave
'Impossible to sleep': A night in sweltering France, in search of a cool spot
description: With a heatwave bearing down, the French have taken to living after dark, chasing relief from the sun's lingering grip. From Paris to Saint-Malo, by way of Orléans, parks and pools stirred to life as soon as the day ended.

headline: French tolerance remains strong, but racism and antisemitism still run deep, report finds
description: In its annual report released on Thursday, the National Consultative Commission on Human Rights expresses concern about the persistence of xenophobic prejudices in French society, particularly among the younger generation.

headline: How cities in Africa are adapting to intense and deadly heat
description: Reflective paints, green roofs, work breaks: Many African countries are seeking innovative ways to cope with increasingly extreme temperatures.
